title: A resonator assembly and filter
abstract: Aspects and embodiments provide a reconfigurable resonator assembly comprising: a resonator enclosure which defines a resonator cavity, a signal feed and a resonant structure. The resonant structure is located within the resonator cavity and arranged to receive a signal from the signal feed. The resonant structure comprises: a tuning member and first and second elongate elements having an overlap region along their length. The first and second elongate elements are arranged to define a volume in said overlap region between an inner surface of one of the elongate elements and an outer surface of the other of the elongate elements. The first and second concentric elements are reconfigurable within the cavity between : a first position in which a first volume is defined in the overlap region and the resonant structure is configured to resonate within the cavity at a first frequency; and a second position in which a second volume is defined in the overlap region and the resonant structure is configured to resonate within the cavity at a second frequency. Aspects and embodiments may also provide a filter including one or more reconfigurable resonator assemblies. Aspects and embodiments may support provision of a miniaturised coaxial resonator which is configured to simultaneously achieve frequency tenability whilst retaining a high quality factor and high power handling ability.
claims:
Claims 1. Areconfigurable resonator assembly comprising: a resonator enclosure which defines a resonator cavity, a signal feed and a resonant structure, said resonant structure being located within the resonator cavity and arranged to receive a signal from said signal feed; said resonant structure comprising: a tuning member and first and second elongate elements, said first and second elongate elements having an overlap region along their length and arranged to define a volume in said overlap region between an inner surface of one of said elongate elements and an outer surface of the other of said elongate elements; said first and second elements being reconfigur able within said cavity between: a first position in which a first volume is defined in said overlap region and said resonant structure is configured to resonate within said cavity at a first frequency; and a second position in which a second volume is defined in said overlap region and said resonant structure is configured to resonate within said cavity at a second frequency. 2. Areconfigurable resonator assembly according to claim 1, wherein said first and second elements are rotatable with respect to each other, and reconfiguring between said first and second positions comprises: rotation of said one of said first and second element with respect to the other. 3. Areconfigurable resonator assembly according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein reconfiguring between said first and second positions comprises: changing a length of said overlap region. 4. A reconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein reconfiguring between first and second positions comprises: changing a distance between said inner surface of one of said elongate elements and said outer surface of the other of said elongate elements in said overlap region. 5. Areconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein at least one of said inner surface of one of said elongate elements and said outer surface of the other of said elongate elements has a non-uniform radius in said overlap region. 6. Areconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein a portion of both said inner surface of one of said elongate elements and said outer 

 surface of the other of said elongate elements has a non-uniform radius in said overlap region. 7. A reconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein at least one of said inner surface of one of said elongate elements and said outer surface of the other of said elongate elements is substantially elliptical in cross section in said overlap region. 8. A reconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein a portion of both said inner surface of one of said elongate elements and said outer surface of the other of said elongate elements is substantially elliptical in cross section in said overlap region. 9. A reconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein at least one of said inner surface of one of said elongate elements and said outer surface of the other of said elongate elements is a polygon in cross section in said overlap region. 10. A reconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein a portion of both said inner surface of one of said elongate elements and said outer surface of the other of said elongate elements is a polygon in cross section in said overlap region. 11. A reconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein at least one of said inner surface of one of said elongate elements and said outer surface of the other of said elongate elements includes at least one opening in said overlap region. 12. A reconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein a portion of both said inner surface of one of said elongate elements and said outer surface of the other of said elongate elements includes at least one opening in said overlap region. 13. A reconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein at least one of said first and second elongate elements comprises a hollow portion into which a portion of the other of said first and second elongate elements extends. 14. A reconfigurable resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein said first and second elements extend into said cavity from opposite surfaces of said resonator enclosure. 15. A filter comprising: a plurality of resonator assemblies, at least one of said resonator assemblies comprising a resonator assembly according to any preceding claim, said filter comprising an input resonator assembly and an output resonator assembly arranged such that a signal received at said input resonator assembly passes through said plurality of resonator assemblies and is output at said output resonator assembly; an input feed line configured to transmit a signal to an input resonator member of said input resonator assembly such that said signal excites said input resonator member, said plurality of resonator assemblies being arranged such that said signal is transferred between said corresponding plurality of resonator members to an output resonator member of said output resonator assembly; an output feed line for receiving said signal from said output resonator member and outputting said signal.
